name: mcp-ssh-manager
description: MCP SSH Server: 37 tools for remote SSH management | Claude Code & OpenAI Codex | DevOps automation, backups, database operations, health monitoring
readme_summary: MCP SSH Manager is a Node.js server that allows AI models like Claude Code and OpenAI Codex to manage remote SSH connections. It facilitates executing commands, transferring files, and automating DevOps tasks on multiple servers through the Model Context Protocol.
key_features: Remote SSH server management via Model Context Protocol (MCP)
Compatibility with Claude Code and OpenAI Codex
primary_use_case: Enabling AI models to securely and efficiently manage and automate tasks on remote SSH servers.
cover_image_prompt: A sophisticated AI assistant, visualized as a wise owl with glowing digital eyes, perched on a network of interconnected servers. The owl's talons are gently manipulating glowing threads of data that flow into and out of various server icons, representing command execution and file transfers. Small, stylized screens around the owl display snippets of code and server status indicators. The background is a dark, starry sky with subtle circuit board patterns. The repository name 'MCP SSH Manager' is subtly integrated into a digital banner. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a futuristic, clean aesthetic and a color palette of blues, purples, and glowing oranges.

name: terminal-use
description: Headless virtual terminal for AI agents. Spawn apps, read the screen, send keystrokes.
readme_summary: terminal-use (`tu`) is a Rust-based headless virtual terminal emulator designed for AI agents. It allows AI agents to spawn and interact with command-line applications in a simulated terminal environment, enabling them to read screen output and send keystrokes without needing a graphical interface or X server.
key_features: Headless virtual terminal for AI agents
No GUI, X server, or display needed
Configurable terminal size and TERM environment variable
primary_use_case: To provide AI agents with a functional, headless terminal environment to interact with command-line applications, enabling complex tasks like navigating file systems, running text-based games, or interacting with ncurses UIs.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled puppeteer, representing the AI agent, masterfully manipulating intricate, glowing strings connected to a classic, old-fashioned terminal screen. The screen displays a complex, interactive text-based game like NetHack, showing the game's output. The puppeteer's hands are depicted as abstract digital constructs, sending precise keystrokes (visualized as small, glowing data packets) into the terminal. The background is a dimly lit, abstract digital space, with subtle lines of code and data flowing around the scene. The overall mood is one of precise control and unseen intelligence driving a complex interface. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on glowing elements and clear visual separation between the agent and the terminal.

name: openclaude
description: Open Claude Is Open-source coding-agent CLI for OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama, Codex, GitHub Models, and 200+ models via OpenAI-compatible APIs.
readme_summary: OpenClaude is an open-source, terminal-first coding agent CLI that unifies access to over 200 AI models, including OpenAI, Gemini, and local options like Ollama. It provides a consistent workflow for developers to interact with various AI backends for coding tasks, leveraging features like slash commands, agents, and streaming output.
key_features: Unified CLI for multiple AI model providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama, GitHub Models, Codex, etc.)
Terminal-first workflow with prompts, tools, agents, MCP, and slash commands
Support for OpenAI-compatible APIs, local servers, and Apple Silicon backends
Streaming output for real-time responses and tool progress
Tool-driven coding workflows including bash, file operations, grep, glob, and web tools
Provider profile management for easy setup and switching
primary_use_case: To provide developers with a single, consistent command-line interface for interacting with a wide variety of AI coding models, both cloud-based and local, streamlining their development workflow.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan, representing the developer, standing at a central workbench in a vibrant, organized workshop. Instead of traditional tools, the artisan is using a magical, multi-faceted prism that refracts a single beam of light (representing code prompts) into numerous distinct streams, each flowing into a different specialized AI 'forge' (visualized as glowing portals or unique workstations). Each forge represents a different AI model provider (e.g., one might have a subtle 'G' for Gemini, another an 'O' for Ollama, a cloud icon for OpenAI). The artisan is seamlessly directing these streams, receiving perfectly crafted code snippets and solutions back from each forge, which are then neatly organized on the workbench. The overall mood is one of efficient, powerful, and unified creation. Style: 3D isometric illustration, clean lines, bright and optimistic color palette, subtle digital particle effects emanating from the prism and forges.

name: neko
description: A self hosted virtual browser that runs in docker and uses WebRTC.
readme_summary: Neko is a self-hosted virtual browser running in Docker, utilizing WebRTC for secure and private internet access from anywhere. It allows multiple users to access a shared browser environment simultaneously, making it suitable for collaboration, remote work, and shared viewing experiences like watch parties and interactive presentations.
key_features: Suitable for watch parties and presentations
primary_use_case: To provide a secure, self-hosted, and accessible virtual browser environment for private browsing, collaboration, and shared media experiences.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, friendly cat wearing a VR headset, sitting comfortably on a cloud. From the cat's paws, streams of data flow into a holographic browser window floating in front of it, displaying a vibrant, dynamic webpage. Around the cat, smaller, transparent screens show diverse users interacting with the same browser content, some giving a thumbs-up, others pointing. Subtle glowing lines connect the cat to these user screens, symbolizing WebRTC streaming. The background is a soft, gradient sky with faint binary code patterns. The overall mood is secure, connected, and accessible. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic and bright, inviting colors.

name: VSRFI-ComfyUI
description: Custom ComfyUI node that combines VSR + VFI and allows streaming processing for arbitrary video length.
readme_summary: This ComfyUI custom node enhances video processing by combining Video Super Resolution (VSR) with Frame Interpolation (VFI). It supports arbitrary video lengths through smart tiling and stream processing, allowing for memory-efficient upscaling and frame rate doubling.
key_features: Video Super Resolution using Flash VSR
Frame Interpolation with GIMM-VFI, RIFE, or FILM
Smart Tile Processing for VRAM optimization
Stream Processing for arbitrary video lengths
primary_use_case: To upscale and enhance the frame rate of long videos within the ComfyUI environment, overcoming memory limitations.
cover_image_prompt: A cinematic scene depicting a vintage film projector seamlessly transforming a grainy, low-frame-rate black and white film reel into a vibrant, ultra-smooth, high-definition movie. The projector itself is subtly integrated with glowing digital circuits and the repository name 'VSRFI-ComfyUI' etched onto its side. Digital particles representing enhanced resolution and interpolated frames flow from the projector's lens onto a large screen, which displays a crystal-clear, fluid animation. The background is a dimly lit, cozy cinema hall, emphasizing the transformation from old to new. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a warm, inviting color palette and a focus on the magical transition of the video.
